text.title: GCP Cloud Storage Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `GCP Cloud Storage`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Production buckets MUST enable uniform bucket-level access; per-object ACLs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Per-object ACLs make IAM auditing intractable; uniform access binds policy at the bucket level only.

rule.statement: Every bucket MUST declare a lifecycle policy with at least one rule transitioning data to a colder class or deleting it.
rule.rationale: Without lifecycle, hot-class storage cost grows unboundedly; lifecycle is the dominant FinOps lever.

rule.statement: Production buckets containing customer or financial data MUST use CMEK with rotation ≤ 90 days.
rule.rationale: Google-managed encryption is opaque; CMEK gives the org a kill-switch + rotation cadence.

rule.statement: Buckets holding system-of-record data MUST enable either Object Versioning or a Retention Policy ≥ 30 days.

rationale.source: GCP Object Versioning docs, GCP Retention Policy docs

rule.rationale: Deletion is irreversible without versioning or retention lock; either control prevents accidental loss.

rule.statement: Buckets MUST have Public Access Prevention enforced unless the bucket is an explicit static-website root and is documented in `allow_public_buckets`.
rule.rationale: Default-private prevents the most common GCS data-leak class (over-permissive ACL).

rule.statement: If `Applies If` preconditions fail OR any `Skip If` trigger fires, do not run this methodology; document skip rationale.
